title: 2026 World Cup: Asian teams maintain unbeaten streak and Japanese fans praised
summary: At the start of the tournament, Asian teams tally wins and draws, while Japanese fans stand out for their exemplary behavior and controversies arise such as North Korea's censorship.

title: Magnitude 5.5 earthquake shakes the Tokyo metropolitan area
summary: The earthquake of weak intensity 5 in Gunma and Saitama prefectures disrupted train services and prompted calls for caution in the Kanto region.

title: Bank of Japan raises interest rate to 1%, highest in decades
summary: The decision, taken by a majority amid inflationary pressures, marks the highest rate since 1995 and generates expectations of future increases.

title: Iran and the United States sign a memorandum to cease hostilities
summary: The agreement, mediated by the international community, provides for the start of peace negotiations and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, with mixed reactions from Israel.

title: G7 summit debates security, trade, and crisis in the Middle East
summary: The G7 leaders, with participation of Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi, discuss cooperation on critical minerals, the normalization of Ormuz, and the situation in Ukraine and Iran.

title: Seibu Lions win the NPB interleague championship for the first time
summary: With an impressive pitching record and the offense of Tazawa Natsuo, the Lions prevail in the interleague league.

title: Japan begins negotiations for an economic agreement with South America
summary: The government seeks to secure oil and critical minerals through an Economic Partnership Agreement with countries in the region.

title: Japan advances law that penalizes destruction of the national flag
summary: The bill, which has support but also criticism for lack of justification, is headed for approval in Parliament.

title: Default rate falls in NHK license fee payment for the first time in six years
summary: Stricter collection measures have managed to reduce the number of households that do not pay the public broadcaster's fee.

title: Japan's Supreme Court rules that gains from foreign currency exchange are taxable
summary: The court demands that legislation be enacted to make explicit the taxation of exchange rate differences in foreign currency transactions.

title: Tokyo considers resuming bear hunting after two decades due to increase in incidents
summary: Given the increase in sightings and damage, metropolitan authorities evaluate allowing controlled bear hunting.

title: The Nikkei 225 index surpasses 70,000 points for the first time in history
summary: The Japanese stock market reaches a record driven by peace expectations and the Bank of Japan's rate hike.

title: BoJ's rate hike makes mortgages more expensive for young Japanese
summary: The increase in the official interest rate increases the financial burden of variable-rate mortgage loans, affecting younger households.

title: Eight dead in B-52 bomber crash in California
summary: The aircraft crashed during a test mission in the desert, with no survivors reported.

title: Shohei Ohtani leads the first round of voting for the MLB All-Star Game
summary: The Japanese phenomenon is the most voted player in the initial phase of the selection of the Major League All-Star team.

title: President of Fujitsu resigns over inappropriate conduct with a woman
summary: The resignation comes after the executive's improper behavior was revealed, marking a corporate scandal at the tech giant.

title: Emperor Naruhito visits the royal palace museum in the Netherlands
summary: The Japanese monarch continues his official Dutch visit touring the royal palace facilities.

title: FBI foils a plot to attack a martial arts event at the White House
summary: U.S. authorities arrested several suspects who were planning an attack during a tournament at the presidential residence.

title: Ishin Party proposes reviewing Japan's non-nuclear principles
summary: The opposition party raises a debate on the three pillars that prohibit possessing, producing, or allowing nuclear weapons in the country.

title: 450-year-old sacred tree falls at Shimogamo Shrine in Kyoto
summary: A historic pine tree fell in the World Heritage forest, without causing casualties but with great symbolic impact.
